_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Branch-base drift across in-flight items — chore commit ships partial state to siblings

steps.description: Pick option (b) or (c) from the design (operator preference: b — narrow chore commit). Implement so item B's worktree does not inherit item A's partial state. Add daemon log line at worktree-create showing detected in-flight siblings and how they were handled.
Per-agent review of S01: option chosen + justified, single-item happy path preserved, no behavioural change to merges.
Author tests/integration/test_branch_base_drift.py — reproduction test (two in-flight items, B's worktree clean of A's drift) + happy-path regression (single item, no change).
Per-agent review of S03 tests: semantic assertions, both ACs covered, no live-DB usage.
Cross-agent final review of S01..S04: scope discipline, no merge-path regressions, log line shape stable.
Self-assessment via iw-item-analyze — focus on whether this CR's own run was free of carry-over fixes (it should be, given there are no in-flight siblings touching the same files at design time).
